data.narrative:
AST SpaceMobile operates in Space & Aerospace as a publicly traded company. The comparable universe consists of 4 same-sector, same-stage peers: Rocket Lab ($37.0B), Planet Labs ($7.0B), Firefly Aerospace ($6.3B), and Astroscale ($900M). The peer set spans a wide valuation range of $900M to $37.0B, reflecting heterogeneity in scale and market positioning within the sector.

Peer-median valuation anchors the primary triangulation method. The median across the 4 public Space & Aerospace comparables is $6.7B. A revenue-based P/S approach is not applicable here, as disclosed revenue figures are unavailable. The Public-Comp Anchoring method, which would leverage the sector median P/S multiple of 53.2x across 6 public tickers (RKLB, PL, LUNR, ASTS, SATL), cannot be executed without AST SpaceMobile's current revenue.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $5.7B to $7.6B, centered on a $6.7B median derived solely from peer multiples. Key uncertainties include the wide dispersion among comparables—a 41x spread between the highest and lowest peer—and reliance on a single methodological input. Absent revenue disclosure, revenue-multiple cross-checks remain unavailable. The narrow applicability of valuation methods reflects standard constraints in public-company analysis when financial detail is limited.
